ground_truth.title: How to use promises
with: Promises are the foundation of asynchronous programming in modern JavaScript.
This is called promise chaining and means we can avoid ever-increasing levels of indentation
To support error handling, Promise objects provide a catch() method.
The async keyword gives you a simpler way to work with asynchronous promise-based code.
Many modern Web APIs are promise-based, including WebRTC, Web Audio API
without: Was this page helpful to you?
